sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient is a woman in her mid-life who has been experiencing headaches for years, starting in her teenage years.
She reports that the headaches were initially related to her menstrual periods but have since become more frequent and severe.
She describes the headaches as a pulsing sensation on the right side of her head, behind her eye.
She also reports that her headaches worsen with exercise, particularly when jogging.
Additionally, she has been experiencing a ringing in her ears and blurred vision, which is intermittent but affects the side of her vision.
Past medical history: [NOT MENTIONED]
Review of systems: The patient reports numbness or tingling in her hands, feet, or face, but denies any such symptoms.
Current medications: [NOT MENTIONED]
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: The patient's heart exam is normal, with no murmurs, rubs, or gallops.
The funduscopic exam shows papilledema, or swelling behind the eye.
The neurological exam is normal, with intact cranial nerves one through twelve and normal cranial reflexes.
The musculoskeletal exam shows a normal gait.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's symptoms are concerning for idiopathic intracranial hypertension, a condition characterized by increased pressure in the brain without a known cause.
Problem list: Idiopathic intracranial hypertension; confirmed; Idiopathic intracranial hypertension; suspected; Blurred vision; suspected; Tinnitus; suspected.
Plan:
I plan to obtain an MRI to confirm the diagnosis of idiopathic intracranial hypertension.
I will also perform a spinal tap to evaluate the pressure in the brain and to help alleviate symptoms.
Additionally, I will refer the patient to a neuro-ophthalmologist for a complete eye exam.
Follow-up: The patient will have an MRI, which will require pre-approval from her insurance company.
Once pre-approval is obtained, the patient will be contacted to schedule the MRI.
